unit.name: Multilayer RNNs and compact notation
unit.description: Explains stacked recurrent layers and shorthand matrix notation before introducing LSTMs.
unit.summary: Practical RNNs are often multilayer, analogous to stacked CNN or fully connected layers [ts=3408s] [ts=3422s]. In a multilayer RNN, each layer’s hidden state depends on that same layer’s previous time step, while upper layers take lower-layer outputs as their inputs, forming a time-by-depth grid [ts=3430s] [ts=3463s]. Computing later high-layer states requires many earlier hidden states, which makes training involved and inefficient [ts=3483s]. The lecture introduces LSTMs as a key 1990s RNN variant designed to alleviate vanilla RNN disadvantages [ts=3502s] [ts=3521s]. It also reviews the vanilla update with W_hh and W_xh and explains that authors often combine them into one larger W by stacking vectors, although the conceptual blocks remain distinct [ts=3537s] [ts=3562s] [ts=3571s]. Weights are shared through time within a layer, but each stacked layer has its own weight set [ts=3641s].

key_points.text: Multilayer RNNs stack recurrent layers over both depth and time.
Within a layer, h_t depends on that layer’s h_{t-1}; upper layers use lower-layer outputs.
Computing late high-layer states requires many earlier hidden states.
W_hh and W_xh can be notated as one larger W over stacked vectors.
Weights are shared through time within a layer, but different layers have separate weights.

kp_catalog.name: Multilayer RNNs
kp_catalog.description: Stacking recurrent layers over depth while sharing weights over time within each layer.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because multilayer RNNs clarifies stacking recurrent layers over depth while sharing weights over time within each layer, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: LSTM gates and cell state
kp_catalog.description: Gated recurrent architecture that selectively writes, forgets, outputs, and preserves information.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because lSTM gates and cell state formalizes how gated recurrent architecture that selectively writes, forgets, outputs, and preserves information, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
